field.label: Enable VPN Link
field.help: Enable automatic NAT, DNS, and routing rules for WireGuard VPN clients.

field.label: Client Mode
field.help:
Intranet Only: VPN clients can only access specified LAN subnets. No internet access.
LAN Equivalent: VPN clients are treated like LAN devices — they inherit all existing firewall rules including domain-based gateway routing. Traffic is masqueraded on every exit interface.

field.label: Auto NAT
field.help: Automatically create outbound NAT (SNAT) on every exit interface. Includes loopback NAT for DNS access.

field.label: DNS Sync
field.help: Automatically add WireGuard tunnel subnets to DNS access control (Unbound ACL / AdGuard bind).

field.label: DNS Topology
field.help:
Auto-Detect: Automatically detect if AdGuard Home is installed.
Unbound Only: Plugin syncs Unbound ACL.
AdGuard Home + Unbound: Plugin ensures AdGuard listens on WG IPs and syncs Unbound ACL.

field.label: LAN Interface(s)
field.help:
Comma-separated LAN interface names (e.g. "opt1" or "opt1,opt2").
LAN Equivalent: VPN traffic is masqueraded on these interfaces to inherit their firewall rules.
Intranet Only: NAT is applied on these interfaces for return traffic.

field.label: NAT Interfaces (VPN Gateways)
field.help:
Additional interfaces to auto-generate outbound NAT on. Use this for VPN gateway interfaces (e.g. "ovpnc1,ovpnc2" for OpenVPN clients used as gateways).
WAN and LAN interfaces are always included automatically.

field.label: LAN Subnets (Intranet Mode)
field.help: Comma-separated subnets accessible in Intranet Only mode (e.g. "192.168.1.0/24"). Ignored in LAN Equivalent mode.
